title: how the web works
question: What happens first when you type a URL into your browser and press Enter?
options: The browser renders the page|The server sends CSS files|The browser sends an HTTP request to the server|JavaScript runs on the page
answer: The browser sends an HTTP request to the server

title: how the web works
question: What does the HTTP GET method do?
options: Sends data to the server|Requests a resource from the server|Deletes a file on the server|Updates an existing record
answer: Requests a resource from the server

answer: A response containing the requested resources

options: Server error|Page not found|The resource has been permanently redirected|Successful request
answer: The resource has been permanently redirected

title: how the web works
question: Why does the browser make dozens of requests when loading a single page like jumia.com.ng?
options: The server is slow|Each resource (HTML CSS JS images fonts) requires a separate request|The browser is broken|DNS returns multiple pages
answer: Each resource (HTML CSS JS images fonts) requires a separate request

question: What is the difference between an inline script and an external script?

options: Naija 42 on one line|Only Naija|Naija on the first line and 42 on the second line|An error
answer: Naija on the first line and 42 on the second line

title: running javascript in a web page
question: If the Console is empty after loading a page with a linked script what is the most likely cause?
options: DevTools is broken|The script file name in the src attribute does not match the actual file name|The browser does not support JavaScript|The HTML is invalid
answer: The script file name in the src attribute does not match the actual file name

question: Why is the script tag placed before the closing body tag rather than in the head?
options: So it loads faster from the server|So that all HTML elements exist before JavaScript tries to access them|Because the head tag cannot contain script tags|It makes no difference
answer: So that all HTML elements exist before JavaScript tries to access them

options: b.js|They run simultaneously|a.js|It is random

title: running javascript in a web page
question: A junior developer writes console.log("Welcome") expecting the text to appear on the page. Why does it not appear?
options: The browser is offline|console.log outputs to the developer Console not to the rendered HTML page|The string is too short|JavaScript cannot display text
answer: console.log outputs to the developer Console not to the rendered HTML page

title: running javascript in a web page
question: What is the main advantage of using an external .js file instead of an inline script for a large project?
options: External files run faster|External files can be reused across multiple HTML pages and keep code organised|Inline scripts do not support console.log|External files are required by all browsers
answer: External files can be reused across multiple HTML pages and keep code organised
